title: Austria Wants to Keep Kids Off Social Media
summary: Austria is planning a new rule to stop children under 14 from using social media. This means apps like TikTok or Instagram would not be allowed for young kids. The government says this will protect children from bad things online and too much screen time. They want to use special age checks to make sure only older people can sign up. Other countries like Australia and France are doing similar bans for kids. Austria also plans to teach kids in school about using media safely. The rule needs approval from lawmakers before it starts.

keywords.explanation: apps and websites where people share things online, like TikTok or Instagram
a rule that says you cannot do something
a way to check how old someone is before they can use something
the amount of time spent looking at phones or computers

options: To protect them from bad content
To make them use more apps
To stop them from playing outside
questions.correct_answer: To protect them from bad content

Which country was the first to ban kids under 16 from social media?
What else will Austria do besides the ban?
options: Teach kids about media in school

questions.correct_answer: Teach kids about media in school

questions.question: Who needs to approve Austria's plan before it starts?

background_read: Social media is popular with kids, but some countries worry it can be harmful. Austria is joining others like Australia and France to make rules keeping young children off these apps. They want to use age checks and teach safety in schools to help kids.
Article_Structure: WHO: Austria's government and other countries like Australia and France
WHAT: Plan to ban children under 14 from using social media
WHERE: In Austria and other places around the world
WHY: To protect kids from harmful content and too much screen time

perspectives.description: The ban will keep children safe from addiction and bad things online.
